title: Battery Boom Is Upending Australian Power Market Rules (Podcast)
summary: Australia is becoming a proving ground for the future of electricity markets. With renewables supplying a large share of generation and batteries being deployed at unprecedented scale, the country is offering an early look at how power systems operate when storage becomes a central part of the grid. Yet batteries’ success story is now ushering in new challenges, as growing competition erodes the arbitrage opportunities that drove the sector’s early growth. summary: US stocks staged a modest rebound as investors looked ahead to Micron Technology Inc.’s results for the next read on whether artificial intelligence spending is living up to expectations.

summary: Europe’s electricity system is under mounting pressure from an intense heat wave, with extreme swings in power prices as soaring temperatures drive up demand and curb output.

title: Indonesia Plans to Boost Nickel Output, Fueling Price Drop
summary: Indonesia is poised to allow a significant rise in nickel-mine production later this year, providing relief for local smelters while weighing on global prices of the battery metal.

title: Stocks Rise Ahead of Micron Earnings; US, Iran Offer Conflicting Claims | Bloomberg Brief 6/24/2026
summary: US equity futures edge higher ahead of earnings results from Micron, after the Nasdaq 100 closed down by more than 3%. SK Hynix plans to raise $30 billion in a US listing to meet memory chip demand. Oil extends decline amid ongoing US-Iran talks. Max Kettner of HSBC sees an expiry date to US exceptionalism in the near term. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Tech Stocks Set to Bounce After $1.3 Trillion Rout on AI Jitters
summary: US technology stocks are set for a rebound on Wednesday after concerns around artificial intelligence valuations wiped nearly $1.3 trillion from the market capitalization of Nasdaq 100 companies over the first two days of the week.

title: European Stocks Steady Ahead of Micron Earnings  | The Opening Trade 6/24/2026
summary:
Equity markets steadied after a brief selloff in top-performing technology names, with Micron’s earnings taking on added significance as a test for the rally in artificial intelligence stocks. Micron’s earnings report on Wednesday afternoon is shaping up to be one of the most important in months as investors find themselves suddenly on edge over the sustainability of the AI rally.

The Opening Trade has everything you need to know as markets open across Europe. With analysis you won't find anywhere else, we break down the biggest stories of the day and speak to top guests who have skin in the game. Hosted by Guy Johnson and Tom Mackenzie.
 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: UK Banks Set to Extend Rally Against EU Peers, Berenberg Says
summary: UK bank stocks are well placed to catch up to the loftier valuations of European peers in coming years as they benefit from above-par growth in profitability and stronger capital generation, according to Berenberg analysts.

title: The Debasement Trade Is Unraveling and Kevin Warsh Is One Big Reason
summary: In hindsight, the beginning of the end for the debasement trade can be traced back to Jan. 30.

title: Stock Rout Pauses, Europe Heat Wave Intensifies | The Pulse 6/24/2026
summary:
"The Pulse With Francine Lacqua" is all about conversations with high profile guests in the beating heart of global business, economics, finance and politics. Based in London, we go wherever the story is, bringing you exclusive interviews and market-moving scoops.

Today's guests: Joyce Chang, JPMorgan Global Research Chair; Clemens Fuest, Ifo Institute President; Miljan Gutovic, Holcim CEO; Benoit Bazin, Saint-Gobain CEO.    (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Investor Who Scored 900% Win in 2008 Crisis Has New Big Short Bet
summary: Hedge fund manager Lee Robinson notched a 900% gain during the global financial crisis by turning a $20 million position into $200 million with timely bets against the US subprime mortgage sector.

title: Options Trades Highlight Doubts Over Prospect of US Rate Hikes
summary: Options traders are building up bets that the broader market overreacted to the Federal Reserve’s hawkish pivot when it priced in a series of interest-rate hikes in the US starting later this year.

title: Airbus A380 Aircraft Require Checks for Possible Wing Cracks
summary: The European Union Aviation Safety Agency is asking some operators of Airbus SE A380 jets to perform additional inspections on their aircraft’s wings after identifying possible cracks in the structure.

title: Rheinmetall Sinks as Germany Shelves Major Warship Contract
summary: Rheinmetall AG shares plunged Wednesday as Germany withdrew from a key warships contract, dealing a blow to investor confidence in the defense contractor following years of huge gains.

title: US Exceptionalism Nearing Expiration Date, HSBC’s Kettner Says
summary: Max Kettner, chief multi-asset strategist at HSBC, says investors may want to be “perhaps really leaning a bit more into that European story,” as he sees an August expiration date on US exceptionalism. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Investors Maxing Out AI Trade Feed Stock Market Volatility
summary: Investors are maxing out the AI trade, setting the stock market up for a volatile summer.

title: Gold Drops to Near $4,000 on Stronger Dollar, Fed Rate Outlook
summary: Gold fell for a second day, extending losses to trade near $4,000 an ounce as a stronger US dollar continues to put pressure on prices.

title: US Diesel Prices Fall Under $5, Easing Inflationary Pressure
summary: The cost of diesel in the US fell below $5 a gallon for the first time since mid-March, offering some relief for one of the global economy’s most important fuels.

title: EU Competition Chief Warns Governments Not to Stymie Bank Deals
summary: The European Union’s competition chief has warned nations against overusing tactics such as citing security risks to stymie bank deals needed to boost the economy.

title: South Korea Delays Single-Stock Weekly Options Days Before Start
summary: South Korea has delayed a plan to launch weekly single-stock options on shares including SK Hynix Inc. and Samsung Electronics Co., the latest sign regulators are becoming jittery after wild stock-market swings.

title: Oil Extends Drop as More Tankers Cross Hormuz After Peace Talks
summary: Oil extended declines as more tankers openly cross the Strait of Hormuz, while the US and Iran signal progress toward ending the war.

title: Indonesia Tightens Oversight of Financial Influencers
summary: Indonesia is requiring financial influencers to disclose paid promotions and obtain licenses to recommend assets as Southeast Asia’s largest economy joins a global movement to tighten oversight of the social media personalities.

title: Indian Bonds Rally as RBI Governor Says Rate Hike Talk Premature
summary: India’s benchmark bond yields and swap rates fell to their lowest levels in three months as the nation’s central bank Governor pushed back against expectations for tighter monetary policy.

title: Indian State Lenders Said to Seek $2 Billion in Overseas Loans
summary: Some Indian state-run financial institutions are planning to raise overseas loans of as much as $2 billion using a central bank facility for borrowing at concessional rates, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Korea’s Surging Equity Volatility Draws Meme-Stock Comparisons
summary: The volatility in South Korea’s benchmark equity index has become so extreme that investors and analysts are likening the market’s surging intra-day swings to the meme-stock frenzy.

title: MSCI Warns It May Review Turkey Markets Classification
summary: MSCI Inc. warned Turkey that it may review its index classification if the country doesn’t improve shareholder transparency.

title: Paramount’s $110 Billion Warner Deal Faces EU Moviemaker Test
summary: Paramount Skydance Corp.’s $110 billion takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery Inc. is raising questions over filmmakers’ ability to reach audiences in the European Union, according to the bloc’s antitrust chief.

title: Copper Holds Losses as Dollar and Hawkish Fed Pressure Metals
summary: Copper and industrial metals largely held Tuesday’s slump as a stronger dollar and the Federal Reserve’s hawkish stance weighed on the demand outlook.

title: Oil Tanker Booked in Persian Gulf at 897% of Benchmark Rate
summary: One of the world’s biggest operators of supertankers has provisionally booked a vessel to transport oil from the Persian Gulf to India at nearly nine times benchmark rates, a price that reflects the shortage of avalable ships in the area.

title: CATL Sees Its Sodium Batteries in at Least 10,000 EVs This Year
summary: Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. Ltd. expects 10,000 to 20,000 electric vehicles to carry its sodium-ion batteries this year, a senior official for the Chinese company said.
